pr.title: Group the wiki TU list into progress sections
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Updated tools/wiki_tu.py so the generated Translation Units wiki page is no longer one flat table. The script now buckets TUs into `.text in progress`, `.text matched - data / linking left`, and `Fully linked` sections, using `metadata.complete` as the canonical fully linked signal and `.text` match percentage to split remaining work. Active sections now show separate `.text %` and `data %` columns, while the obsolete combined percent/checkmark completion column was removed. The PR also cleaned formatting by using non-breaking spaces in humanfriendly size strings and normalizing Discord assignees to exactly one leading `@`.

key_files.role: Only changed file; restructures Translation Units wiki output, row formatting, bucketing, percent display, and assignee normalization.

what_changed: `wiki_tu.py write` changed from emitting one flat TU table to printing three progress sections: `.text in progress`, `.text matched - data / linking left`, and `Fully linked`.
Added helper functions inside `write`: `friendly_size`, `percent_cell`, `file_of`, `assignee_cells`, `percent_cells`, `row_full`, and `row_slim` to isolate formatting and row-generation logic.
Introduced TU bucketing: `metadata.complete` entries go to `linked`; non-complete TUs with `matched_code_percent >= 100` go to `data_left`; all other non-complete TUs go to `in_progress`.
Active tables now include separate `.text %` and `data %` columns, making code completion distinct from data/linking completion.
Fully linked rows use a slimmer table with `File|.text %|data %`, omitting matched/total sizes and assignee columns.
Removed the old combined `%` plus `:heavy_check_mark:` / `:x:` completion column; completion is now represented by section membership.
Section counts are printed on their own line below each header instead of being embedded in the header text, preserving stable wiki anchor URLs as counts change.
Size strings from `humanfriendly.format_size` now replace spaces with ` ` and replace `bytes` with `B`, preventing units such as `1.18 KB` from wrapping inside table cells.
Discord assignee cells are normalized to exactly one leading `@` with `@{assignee['discord'].lstrip('@')}`.
Assignee columns remain at the same positions in active/full tables so `read_wiki` can continue round-tripping existing Discord and GitHub assignee data.
smart_moves: Used `metadata.complete` as the highest-priority done criterion because the PR states it drives the official completed-unit count; this avoids treating 100% `.text` as fully finished when data or linking remains.
Separated `.text %` and `data %`, which makes TUs with complete code but incomplete data visibly different from genuinely complete TUs.
Kept section counts below headers so generated wiki anchors remain stable and external links to sections do not churn as TU counts change.
Factored repeated table logic into small local helpers, reducing duplicated formatting code and making future wiki output changes safer.
Preserved active-table assignee column indices, explicitly maintaining compatibility with `read_wiki` round-tripping.
Normalized Discord handles on write rather than relying on users to enter them consistently.
Used `—` for percent cells when a TU has no total for that section, avoiding misleading `0%` or divide-by-zero behavior.
decomp_lessons: Progress reporting should distinguish code matching, data matching, and final linking; a single percent/checkmark column can hide actionable remaining work.
For Melee TU tracking, `metadata.complete` should be treated as the canonical fully linked/done flag, not merely `matched_code_percent == 100`.
Generated wiki pages used for coordination should optimize for the currently actionable work first; moving fully linked TUs to a bottom/slim section makes unfinished work easier to find.
When changing generated wiki table schemas, preserve columns consumed by parsers such as `read_wiki` unless the parser is updated at the same time.
Avoid putting volatile counts in Markdown section headers if users may deep-link to those headers; put counts below headers to keep anchors stable.
Human-readable size formatting in narrow Markdown table cells may need non-breaking spaces to avoid awkward line wrapping.

naming_conventions.meaning: Section for TUs whose `matched_code_percent` is below 100 and are not marked complete.
Section for non-complete TUs whose `.text` code is fully matched but data matching or linking remains.
Section for TUs where `metadata.complete` is set.
Displayed code matching percentage derived from matched/total code measures or reported `matched_code_percent`.
Displayed data matching percentage derived from matched/total data measures or reported `matched_data_percent`.

assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly-level matching tactic was changed in this PR; the matching-related impact is in tooling presentation of TU `.text`, data, and linked completion status.
The script uses `matched_code_percent >= 100` only to identify the intermediate state where code is matched but the TU is not yet marked complete.
Future decomp progress tooling can reuse the three-state distinction: code still in progress, code matched but data/linking left, and fully linked.

review_feedback.feedback: Remove emoji from headers because they do not render in the table of contents.
review_feedback.outcome: The final section headers use plain text without emoji.

review_feedback.feedback: Place counts below headers so section hyperlink URLs remain consistent.
review_feedback.outcome: Counts are printed on a separate line after each `##` header.

review_feedback.feedback: Do not use a `details` tag; linked TUs are at the bottom and rendering the tables should not be a browser problem.
review_feedback.outcome: No `details` wrapper appears in the diff excerpt.

review_feedback.feedback: Replace spaces in humanfriendly size strings with ` `.
Discord usernames should start with exactly one `@`, whether the parsed version had one or not.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modify tools/wiki_tu.py to see whether the three progress sections or table schemas changed again.
Search for read_wiki parser assumptions about assignee column indices before changing Translation Units wiki table layout.
Look for PRs that mark TUs `metadata.complete` to understand how fully linked status is maintained.
Check generated Translation-Units wiki output for whether `.text %` and `data %` remain the primary progress signals.
